title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
summary: TEN Framework is an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents with support for streaming audio, interruptions, VAD, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. It uses graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines to enable natural conversational interactions where agents can be interrupted and handle edge cases that typical STT-LLM-TTS pipelines fail to address.
writeup: This Better Stack video covers TEN Framework, an open-source framework for building real-time voice AI agents. It supports streaming audio, interruptions, voice activity detection, turn detection, STT, LLMs, TTS, avatars, telephony, and multimodal workflows. The architecture relies on graph-based extensions and real-time audio pipelines so that agents can be interrupted mid-response and handle edge cases that standard STT-LLM-TTS pipelines fail to address. The key point is that natural conversation requires interruption handling and turn detection rather than a linear transcribe-generate-speak chain.
